name: rls_policy_always_true
title: RLS Policy Always True
description: Detects RLS policies that use overly permissive expressions like \`USING (true)\` or \`WITH CHECK (true)\` for UPDATE, DELETE, or INSERT operations. SELECT policies with \`USING (true)\` are intentionally excluded as this pattern is often used deliberately for public read access.
detail: Table `public.channel_flags` has an RLS policy `Anyone can flag a channel` for `INSERT` that allows unrestricted access (WITH CHECK clause is always true). This effectively bypasses row-level security for -.

name: rls_policy_always_true
title: RLS Policy Always True
description: Detects RLS policies that use overly permissive expressions like \`USING (true)\` or \`WITH CHECK (true)\` for UPDATE, DELETE, or INSERT operations. SELECT policies with \`USING (true)\` are intentionally excluded as this pattern is often used deliberately for public read access.
detail: Table `public.channels` has an RLS policy `Admins can do everything on channels` for `ALL` that allows unrestricted access (both USING and WITH CHECK are always true). This effectively bypasses row-level security for authenticated.

name: public_bucket_allows_listing
title: Public Bucket Allows Listing
description: Detects public storage buckets with a broad SELECT policy on `storage.objects`, which allows clients to list all files in the bucket.
detail: Public bucket `film-images` has 1 broad SELECT policy on `storage.objects` (public view film images), allowing clients to list all files. Public buckets don't need this for object URL access and it may expose more data than intended.
